p.seg: To all who shall view or hear this writing Helen the daughter of the late Roger called Porter wishes eternal salvation in the Lord.

Know me in my great need to have sold and granted to my lord Robert de Brus the lord of Annandale one grange with the acre upon which it is built and which stands opposite the dwelling of a certain Patrick of Shelton at the head of the toun of Annan on the way to the gallows in return for which the said Robert de Brus has paid to me a certain amount of money as an advance in my great need and in respect of which money I remain satisfied and paid. For the said lord Robert de Brus and his heirs or his assigns to hold and to have freely, quitly, peaceably, and honourably, without any claim on me or my heirs. He or his heirs are annually to render for it one penny at Christmas for the lighting of St Mary’s at Annan in return for every service and secular demand. I and my heirs shall warrant and protect for ever against anyone the same grange with the acre upon which it is built.
In testimony of this matter I have applied my seal to this writing.
With these witnesses, the lord Robert de Heriz, Ralph de Camera the constable, and many others.
